claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded throughput snapshot for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB) host, covering BOTH the 601-token prefill lane and the short-context 128-token greedy decode lane. Camelid reads above both llama.cpp and MLX-LM in every round of this session in both lanes (prefill 587.3/587.3/587.4 vs MLX-LM 579.3/577.9/577.9 and llama.cpp 543.7/543.7/543.8; decode 29.74/29.85/29.74 vs MLX-LM 29.54/29.13/29.01 and llama.cpp 29.08/29.14/29.22). Margins are +1.6% (prefill) and +2.1% (decode) on session medians — every Camelid round exceeds every comparator round, but the margins are narrow: this is a same-session win on this exact row, not a durable or general claim. Not a model-family, context-sweep, or production-load claim.

host.thermal_note: warm host; rounds alternate runtime order to bound order/thermal bias. All three runtimes read faster on this host in this session than in the prior decode snapshot (llama.cpp tg128 28.7 -> 29.1, MLX-LM 28.7 -> 29.1), so cross-session numbers are not comparable; the claim rests on the same-session rounds only.

workload.model_row: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 (GGUF for camelid and llama.cpp; mlx-community Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-8bit for MLX-LM)
workload.prefill_prompt: 601-token repeated-sentence prompt (' '.join(['The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.'] * 60))
workload.sampling: greedy (temperature 0); camelid decode rides the resident GPU-sampling fast lane (default at temperature 0)
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate two-point decode + 601-token verbose prompt pass (mlx-lm, see mlx-version.txt)

method: Three same-session rounds with alternating runtime order (llama.cpp first in round 2, camelid first in rounds 1 and 3). Per-runtime result is the median across rounds; camelid per-round value is the median of 5 measured iterations (1 unmeasured warmup).

camelid.note: 128 greedy tokens after a short prompt; GPU-side argmax + embedding gather, next graph pre-released

parity: Camelid greedy continuations match the CPU sampling path token-for-token: the 16-token prefill-row continuation in every iteration of every round, and the 128-token decode continuation identical across all 15 decode iterations (output_token_ids in the jsonl logs). The GPU sampler's argmax tie-break and embedding-gather dequant are additionally pinned by unit tests against CPU references.
